name: Event-Driven Architecture
description: An event-driven architecture using asynchronous messaging as the primary integration pattern. Producers publish events to a central message broker (e.g., Kafka, RabbitMQ, NATS), and consumers process them independently. This decoupled pattern enables scalability and resilience but introduces security considerations around message integrity, consumer authorization, and event replay attacks.

zones.name: external
zones.description: External event producers and API consumers that generate events or trigger workflows from outside the organizational boundary. All input is untrusted and must be validated before entering the event pipeline.

zones.name: ingestion
zones.description: The ingestion layer that receives external events, validates schemas, authenticates producers, and publishes validated events to the internal event bus. Acts as a buffer between untrusted sources and the trusted event backbone.

zones.name: event-bus
zones.description: The central event backbone providing durable, ordered event delivery. Contains topics/subjects organized by domain, with schema enforcement and retention policies. The source of truth for all system events.

zones.name: processors
zones.description: Event processing services that consume events, execute business logic, and produce derived events or state changes. Includes both simple consumers and complex event processing (CEP) engines, saga coordinators, and CQRS read-model projectors.

zones.name: data-tier
zones.description: The data persistence layer containing event stores, aggregate state databases, read-model projections, and audit logs. All writes come from authorized processors and are fully auditable via the event stream.

mermaid_template:
flowchart TB
    subgraph external["External (Untrusted)"]
        user[End User]
        webhook[Webhook Source]
        partner[Partner System]
    end
    subgraph ingestion_layer["Ingestion (Semi-Trusted)"]
        apigw(API Gateway)
        receiver(Webhook Receiver)
        validator(Schema Validator)
        dlq([Dead-Letter Queue])
    end
    subgraph bus["Event Bus (Trusted)"]
        broker([Message Broker])
        topic1([Topic: Orders])
        topic2([Topic: Payments])
        topic3([Topic: Notifications])
        schema(Schema Registry)
    end
    subgraph processors_tier["Processors (Trusted)"]
        consumer1(Order Processor)
        consumer2(Payment Processor)
        consumer3(Notification Service)
        saga(Saga Orchestrator)
    end
    subgraph data["Data Tier (Highly Trusted)"]
        eventstore[(Event Store)]
        db[(Aggregate DB)]
        readmodel[(Read Model)]
        audit[(Audit Log)]
    end
    user -->|HTTPS| apigw
    webhook -->|HTTPS| receiver
    partner -->|HTTPS| apigw
    apigw --> validator
    receiver --> validator
    validator -->|Valid| broker
    validator -->|Invalid| dlq
    broker --> topic1
    broker --> topic2
    broker --> topic3
    schema -.-> broker
    topic1 -->|Subscribe| consumer1
    topic2 -->|Subscribe| consumer2
    topic3 -->|Subscribe| consumer3
    consumer1 --> saga
    saga --> topic2
    consumer1 -->|Write| db
    consumer1 -->|Append| eventstore
    consumer2 -->|Write| db
    consumer3 -->|Project| readmodel
    consumer1 -.->|Log| audit
